title: JwtSigningKey
description: HS256 signing key with per-Application binding + rotation. secret_encrypted is KMS-envelope-encrypted; the plaintext HS256 shared secret is NEVER persisted to Postgres and NEVER cached in Redis (memory-only per-process for 60 seconds max). Old keys marked retired_at=now() stay verify-capable through the auth.jwt.rotation_grace_period_days window + 180-day forensic tail before hard delete.

kid.description: Key ID exposed via JWKS. Format: `<issuer>-<year>-<month>-<index>`, e.g. `identity-2026-07-1`. UNIQUE.

application_id.description: FK to applications.id ON DELETE RESTRICT. NULL = global signing key (used for platform-plane JWTs).

algorithm.description: Wave 1a: HS256 only. Wave 1c will add RS256, ES256.

secret_encrypted.description: KMS-envelope-encrypted HS256 shared secret. Base64-encoded. NEVER decrypted to a persistent variable — stack-scoped per signing call.

is_active.description: Only ONE key per (application_id, is_active=true) is signing-capable at a time. Verify-capability extends to retired keys within the grace window.

rotated_at.description: Set when a new active key supersedes this one. Rotation cadence: monthly per RotateJwtSigningKeyJob.

retired_at.description: After this timestamp the key is only used for verification. Cleared 180 days later.

indexes.predicate: is_active = true AND retired_at IS NULL
indexes.name: auth_jwt_signing_keys_one_active_per_app

x-wire.note: Platform-admin plane surface (GET /platform/jwt-signing-keys). Wire projection returns { id, kid, application_id, algorithm, is_active, issued_at, rotated_at, retired_at, is_signing_capable, is_verify_only }. secret_encrypted NEVER returned.
